content.text:
<timestamp>Monday, May 11, 2026, 6:07 PM (UTC-7)</timestamp>
<user_query>
I just tested the workflow of adding a memory, going to the list page, and then capturing another memory. However, all the items from the previous memory were still there. The workflow for capture memory should be clear from the last or any previous memory.
</user_query>

I’ll trace the capture-flow state in the web app and patch it so starting a new memory always opens with a clean slate. I’m first reading the frontend and testing skill guidance, then I’ll locate where capture form state is persisted and add a regression test.
I’ve got the repo testing/UI constraints loaded; next I’m locating the capture page/form state lifecycle to find why old items survive navigation.

input.pattern: capture memory|Capture Memory|memory capture|new memory|memory items|items

content.text: I found the root cause: after save, the draft is cleared in storage but the in-memory capture state is still populated and immediately re-saved on the next step change, so reopening capture reloads the old memory. I’m patching `CapturePage` to reset capture inputs before transition to done/list, then adding a regression test for list → capture again.

content.name: ApplyPatch

input.pattern: export async function getMemories|type MemoriesListResponse|interface MemoriesListResponse

content.name: ApplyPatch

content.text: I’ve implemented the fix and regression coverage; now I’m running the capture test suite to verify the new behavior and then checking lints on edited files.

input.description: Run web capture router regression tests

content.text: The regression test exposed a test-isolation issue (multiple mounted app roots between tests), not a product behavior problem. I’m adding `cleanup()` in `afterEach` so each test runs against a fresh DOM, then rerunning the suite.

content.name: ApplyPatch

input.description: Show diff for capture bug fix

content.text:
Capture flow now resets correctly when starting a new memory after saving.

- Fixed `CapturePage` in `apps/web/src/app/router.tsx` so successful save (and queued offline save) now clears in-memory capture inputs **before** the route transitions, then clears persisted draft storage. This prevents old fields/media from being re-saved and reloaded.
- Added reusable reset helpers in the capture page to keep reset behavior consistent.
- Updated the “Capture another memory” action to use the same reset path and clear stale save status fields.

Also added a regression test in `apps/web/src/app/router.capture.test.tsx`:

- New test covers: save memory → return to list → open capture again.
- Verifies the new capture starts blank (photo step `Continue` is disabled until new photo is added, and meta fields are empty).

Validation run:

- `npm run test -w @memories/web -- src/app/router.capture.test.tsx` ✅ (3/3 passing)
- Lints on edited files ✅ no issues

If you want, I can run the broader web test suite next as a full confidence pass.
